title: Add username normalizer and extend formatter test suite
author: eval-bot

context_units.diff_patch:
--- a/app/utils/formatters.py
+++ b/app/utils/formatters.py
@@ -5,3 +5,9 @@
     """Convert text to a URL-safe slug."""
     text = text.lower().strip()
     return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text).strip("-")
+
+
+def normalize_username(username: str) -> str:
+    """Lowercase, strip surrounding whitespace, remove disallowed characters."""
+    username = username.strip().lower()
+    return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9_.-]", "", username)

--- a/tests/test_formatters.py
+++ b/tests/test_formatters.py
@@ -8,3 +8,7 @@
 
 def test_slugify_special_chars():
     assert slugify("foo!@#bar") == "foo-bar"
+
+
+def test_normalize_username_placeholder():
+    assert True

expected_findings.title: New public function normalize_username added with no test coverage
expected_findings.rationale: normalize_username contains non-trivial character-filtering logic but the test suite has no test for it. Edge cases such as empty strings, unicode input, strings that are all disallowed characters, and leading/trailing dots are untested.

expected_findings.title: Vacuous assertion — test always passes regardless of behaviour
expected_findings.rationale: assert True never fails, even if normalize_username raises an exception or is deleted entirely. The test must call the function and assert its return value against expected output.
